Entries.Notes: schema_extracted.json is derived from docs_page.html (SpectaQL HTML at https://data-api.pathlms.com/) via Python HTML parsing. The SpectaQL renderer embeds the full GraphQL schema from the live server at site-build time. 17 query anchors confirmed; 0 mutation anchors confirmed; 0 subscription anchors confirmed. 93 object type anchors confirmed.

The 'id' field is present on approximately 84% of concrete types by direct schema scan. Many report/result types that lack a bare 'id' field carry either 'userId' (referencing a User.id) or are nested result wrappers (e.g. CourseItemViewReport, WebinarArchiveViewerReport, WebinarCancellationReport, WebinarGuestReport) that aggregate child arrays — these wrapper types derive identity from their parent IDs. The pattern is strong enough to emit universalPK={fieldName:'id'} as the D4 hint. Per-IOF IsPrimaryKey declarations will confirm field-by-field at the IO level.

StructuredOutput.errorMessage: Context creation failed: JsonWebTokenError: jwt must be provided
StructuredOutput.authGate: Confirmed — Apollo Server context runs authenticateAccount() before any operation including introspection
StructuredOutput.conclusion: GraphQL introspection is auth-gated. Runtime DiscoverObjects/DiscoverFields must call /graphql with a valid bearer token. Build-time Declared metadata must come from the credential-free SpectaQL docs page.

Entries.Notes: Confirms two-step auth: introspection gate evidence proves the JWT must be obtained via /api/v1/getToken BEFORE any /graphql call. The auth-gate stack trace (jwtDecode -> authenticateAccount -> ApolloServer.context) is explicit.

AMENDMENT [reviewer]: productId description in id=definition-ProductCatalog reads 'The Id of the Product this Sellable Item belongs to' — a FK relationship to the product, NOT the sellable-item row's own identity. IsPrimaryKey set true->false. The row identity is sellableApiId.

AMENDMENT [reviewer]: sellableApiId description in id=definition-ProductCatalog reads 'The id of the Sellable Item (Product)' — the ProductCatalog row's own id. IsPrimaryKey set false->true. This is the correct soft PK for ProductCatalog.

AMENDMENT [reviewer]: in id=definition-ContentSaleOrderItem, orderId is typed Int! [not ID] with no identity description; it references the sibling Order IO whose PK is id: ID!. ContentSaleOrderItem has no row-identity field in the SDL, so it is a value object with zero PK — content-hash identity fallback is correct. orderId IsPrimaryKey set true->false.
